rationale: Ashok, Dillon, and Yuan (2002) is cited in the core model section, where the authors explicitly state that their specified extended logit model follows that work to jointly estimate latent-variable measurement and structural choice equations. That estimation strategy is central to how the paper tests its hypotheses, so the citation is methodological and integral rather than peripheral. The citation is also used as prior support for the choice-modeling approach in the empirical design discussion.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the extended latent-variable discrete choice framework from Ashok et al. (2002) to model consumers' evaluations of mass-customization configurations. It uses that framework as the main estimation backbone while tailoring the specification to this context (including random-coefficient heterogeneity).
